determination.primary_reasoning: Plan criteria not met; no regulatory carve-out applies; clinical standard does not strongly support surgery.

denial_specifies_criteria.reasoning: The denial notice explicitly identifies the clinical criteria applied: 'CC-SPINE-2024 (Plan Medical Policy: Cervical Spine Surgery)' with specific sections § 2.1 and § 2.2. The notice details each criterion and explains how the clinical facts fail to satisfy them. This specification of which clinical criteria from the plan policy were applied directly satisfies the condition.
cited_facts: case content

denial_specifies_clinical_reason.reasoning: The denial notice explicitly identifies the specific clinical facts that led to the denial: (1) no motor or sensory radicular signs documented, negative Spurling's maneuver, and intact neurological examination inconsistent with radiculopathy required by § 2.1; (2) only 4 weeks of physical therapy versus 6-week requirement, only OTC ibuprofen versus multiple agent classes required, and no interventional procedure attempted as required by § 2.2; and (3) MRI showing no significant stenosis or nerve root impingement. These are specific clinical fact patterns, not merely restatements of criteria.
cited_facts: case content

denial_provides_imr_rights.reasoning: The denial notice includes a section titled 'RIGHT TO INDEPENDENT MEDICAL REVIEW' that explicitly informs the member of their right to request an IMR under California Health & Safety Code § 1374.30 and § 1374.31, provides contact information for the Department of Managed Health Care (phone number and website), and states the 6-month filing deadline. This satisfies the requirement that the denial notice include notice of the member's IMR rights.

denial_factual_basis_correct.reasoning: The denial makes several substantive factual assertions: (1) neurological exam showed no radicular signs with intact strength/reflexes/sensation and negative Spurling's; (2) PT was approximately 4 weeks with patient discontinuation; (3) only ibuprofen documented, no other pharmacotherapy classes; (4) no interventional procedures attempted or documented as contraindicated; (5) MRI showed mild degenerative changes without significant stenosis or nerve root impingement. Each of these factual claims is directly supported by the corresponding case record documents. The denial's factual basis is accurate even though it contains clinical interpretations and policy application.

cervical_radiculopathy_diagnosed.reasoning: The condition requires confirmed cervical radiculopathy with nerve root compression and radicular symptoms (pain, numbness, weakness in dermatomal distribution). The clinical records explicitly document 'No radiculopathy documented,' physical examination shows no dermatomal sensory loss, normal strength (5/5) throughout, negative Spurling's test, and MRI shows no nerve root impingement. The bilateral shoulder girdle pain is non-dermatomal and does not meet the criteria for radicular symptoms.
cited_facts: [extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Chronic axial cervical pain with bilateral shoulder girdle pain. Mild multilevel cervical spondylosis (C4-C5, C5-C6, C6-C7 disc desiccation). No radiculopathy documented. No myelopathy documented. No disc herniation with neurological deficit documented.

[extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Examination dated 2026-03-18 by Dr. Elena Rodriguez: Mental status intact. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Motor: strength 5/5 throughout bilateral upper extremities including deltoid, biceps, triceps, wrist extension, finger flexion, intrinsic hand muscles. Sensation: intact to light touch, pinprick, and proprioception throughout C4-T1 distributions bilaterally. No dermatomal sensory loss documented. Reflexes: biceps 2+ bilaterally, triceps 2+ bilaterally, brachioradialis 2+ bilaterally. No Hoffmann sign. No clonus. Spurling's test negative bilaterally. Shoulder abduction relief sign negative. Range of motion: cervical flexion 50°, extension 40°, lateral rotation 70° bilaterally, lateral flexion 35° bilaterally with end-range discomfort. Tenderness to palpation over bilateral paraspinal muscles C5-T1 level and bilateral trapezii. Adson's maneuver negative.

[extract] case content (confidence 0.80): MRI cervical spine, 2026-01-14: Mild multilevel cervical spondylosis. C4-C5: mild disc desiccation, small posterior bulge, no significant central or foraminal stenosis. C5-C6: moderate disc desiccation, small broad-based posterior disc bulge measuring 1.5mm, no central stenosis, mild bilateral neural foraminal narrowing without nerve root impingement. C6-C7: mild disc desiccation, no significant bulge. No evidence of disc herniation, no central canal stenosis, no severe foraminal stenosis, no nerve root impingement, no cord compression, no myelomalacia signal. Impression: Mild multilevel cervical degenerative changes without significant neural compromise.

cervical_myelopathy_diagnosed.reasoning: The condition requires a confirmed diagnosis of cervical myelopathy with specific symptoms including gait disturbance, bilateral upper extremity weakness, hyperreflexia, positive Hoffman sign, and bowel/bladder dysfunction. The medical record explicitly states 'No myelopathy documented,' the examination shows normal strength (5/5), normal reflexes (2+), negative Hoffman sign, and the MRI shows no cord compression. None of the required myelopathic symptoms are present.

pt_contraindicated_or_futile.reasoning: The condition requires written documentation from the treating physician that physical therapy is contraindicated or unlikely to benefit the member. The treating physician's documentation does not include any explicit statement that physical therapy is contraindicated or unlikely to benefit the member. In fact, the physical therapy records show the patient improved during a 4-week course (pain decreased from 7/10 to 4/10, improved range of motion) before self-discontinuing treatment, suggesting benefit was achieved.

functional_plateau_documented.reasoning: The condition requires both completion of a PT course AND documentation of functional plateau. Here, the member did not complete the planned 6-week PT course (discontinued early), and the treating PT explicitly documented 'continued expected progress' and 'making expected gains' rather than plateau. Both prongs of the condition fail: the course was not completed, and there is no documentation of functional plateau—indeed, the opposite is documented.
cited_facts: [extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Discharge summary, Westside Rehabilitation, 2025-09-02: Patient discontinued before completing planned 6-week course. Final session pain rating 4/10 (initial 7/10). Cervical range of motion improved by approximately 15% bilaterally. Patient declined further sessions. Discharge status: improvement noted, plan of care not completed.

[extract] case content (confidence 0.80): Progress notes consistent with discharge summary. No documentation of plateau, contraindication, or futility. Treating PT documented continued expected progress at time of discontinuation. Notes contain language: 'Patient making expected gains and would benefit from completion of planned care; however, patient elects to discontinue.'

structural_neurological_compromise.reasoning: The condition requires objective evidence of structural neurological compromise that PT cannot address, such as cord compression with neurological deficit or significant herniation causing radiculopathy. The MRI shows no disc herniation, no cord compression, no nerve root impingement, and no central canal stenosis. The physical examination shows normal strength (5/5), intact sensation, normal reflexes, negative Spurling's test, and no documented radiculopathy or myelopathy. The facts document degenerative changes but explicitly state no significant neural compromise exists.

clinical_standard_supports_surgery.reasoning: This case presents axial neck pain without radiculopathy or myelopathy. The neurological examination shows normal strength (5/5), intact sensation with no dermatomal loss, normal reflexes, and negative Spurling's and shoulder abduction relief signs. The MRI shows mild degenerative changes without nerve root impingement or cord compression. Under tier_3_criteria, surgery is not clinically indicated when there is absence of radiculopathy and myelopathy, which matches this presentation exactly.
